shared_goal: Convert technical ideas into the smallest solo-shippable, behavior-changing system and then falsify the design for real-world feasibility before implementation.

target_idea.name: TraceCrumb
target_idea.one_line: A decision-memory system for production incidents that captures and reinforces successful first-response diagnostic behavior under operational uncertainty.
target_idea.attack_vector: Attacks the third variable (weak historical decision memory) to compress the fourth (slow convergence).
target_idea.territory: The window between alert firing and first diagnostic branch selection.
target_idea.core_primitive: First Diagnostic Intention — the moment an engineer states where they think the problem is before any branch is explored.

part_1.name: elite_solo_lean_systems_implementation_engine
part_1.purpose: Compress a technical idea into the smallest deployable loop that can be shipped in under 48 hours on free tier.

minimal_system_definition.description: A Slack bot that captures a single diagnostic intention at incident start, retrieves the three most similar past incidents by keyword/service match, and writes one structured artifact to Postgres when the incident closes.
minimal_system_definition.behavior_change_produced: An engineer on call at 2 AM types one command and receives historically grounded first-check suggestions before exploring any branch. That is the observable behavior change. Everything else is future scope.
minimal_system_definition.kill_criteria: If fewer than 3 incidents are logged in the first two weeks by real on-call engineers, the retrieval loop has no signal and the system is dead.

slack_commands: /tc start <service> <symptom> — opens incident, triggers intention prompt
/tc action <text> — logs one action to the event stream
/tc close — closes incident, generates artifact, updates pattern weights
internal_endpoints: GET /retrieve?service=&symptom= — fetch top-3 similar past incidents
POST /action — log one action with outcome
POST /artifact — generate and persist closing artifact
POST /reinforce — update pattern confidence weights on close
api_surface.constraint: No public API in v1. Slack is the only surface. Web UI is explicitly deferred.

execution_flow: Engineer runs /tc start payments-api latency spike
Bot writes incident row to Postgres, prompts: What is the first thing you think we should check?
Engineer responds. Intention written to intentions table.
GET /retrieve runs deterministic keyword + service match against past incidents. Top 3 returned to Slack.
During incident, engineer runs /tc action checked Redis memory — outcome recorded.
Engineer runs /tc close. Artifact generated: intention vs. outcome delta, successful paths, dead ends.
Pattern weights updated: successful first actions gain score, dead ends decay.
Next similar incident retrieves improved suggestions. Loop compounds.

failure_modes.mode: No historical data at cold start
failure_modes.fix: Retrieval returns empty with explicit message: No prior incidents matched. Your actions will seed future retrieval. Cold start is valid state, not a bug.

failure_modes.mode: Engineers skip /tc action logging during high-stress incidents
failure_modes.fix: Make /tc close the only mandatory step. Artifact is generated from intention + resolution alone if action log is empty. Logging is opt-in, not a gate.

failure_modes.mode: Symptom text too freeform to match reliably
failure_modes.fix: V1 uses service_name + top-3 keyword overlap only. Embeddings are explicitly deferred. Deterministic scoring ships first.

failure_modes.mode: Pattern weights drift toward survivor bias (only closed incidents reinforce)
failure_modes.fix: Accepted in v1. Flag in artifact metadata. Correction is a v2 problem.

first_build_order: 1. Supabase project — incidents, intentions, actions, artifacts, pattern_weights tables created.
2. Slack bot registered. /tc start handler written. Incident row inserted on trigger.
3. Intention prompt sent to Slack thread. Response written to intentions table.
4. GET /retrieve — deterministic service + keyword match against closed incidents. Returns top 3.
5. /tc action handler — appends action rows to incident. Outcome field optional at log time.
6. /tc close handler — artifact generated from incident record + intentions + actions. Written to artifacts table.
7. Pattern reinforcement — on close, confidence scores updated for actions marked useful or root_cause.
8. Deploy to Railway or Render free tier. Slack bot live. First real incident logged.

part_2.name: elite_solo_reality_first_feasibility_checker
part_2.purpose: Destroy weak assumptions and reject designs that cannot be shipped by one solo developer in under 48 hours.

issue_by_issue_diagnosis.issue: LLM layer described in technical direction
issue_by_issue_diagnosis.diagnosis: Symptom normalization and retrieval explanation via LLM adds an async call, prompt engineering, latency, and potential hallucination surface to every incident start. This is not the smallest loop.
issue_by_issue_diagnosis.minimal_fix: Remove LLM entirely from v1. Deterministic keyword matching on service_name + symptom_text is sufficient for retrieval. Artifact summarization is structured template output, not LLM generation. LLM is explicitly deferred to v2.

issue_by_issue_diagnosis.diagnosis: Confidence weight decay and compounding reinforcement is statistically meaningful only after 20–50+ incidents. In 48 hours, zero real incidents will have been logged. The engine has no data to operate on.
issue_by_issue_diagnosis.minimal_fix: Write the schema and the weight update function. Do not build a scoring UI or decay algorithm. A simple increment/decrement on useful vs dead_end outcomes is sufficient. Display the raw count in retrieval output. Call it done.

issue_by_issue_diagnosis.issue: Six audience segments defined in product document
issue_by_issue_diagnosis.diagnosis: Compliance buyers, engineering managers, and enterprise audit trails are not the users who will run /tc start at 2 AM. Building for six personas simultaneously produces a system optimized for none of them.
issue_by_issue_diagnosis.minimal_fix: V1 ships for exactly one persona: the on-call engineer. Every design decision routes through that user and no other. All other segments are noted and deferred.

issue_by_issue_diagnosis.issue: Minimal web UI listed as secondary interface
issue_by_issue_diagnosis.diagnosis: Any web UI — however minimal — requires auth, routing, state management, and deployment configuration. For a solo developer in 48 hours, this is not secondary. It is a second product.
issue_by_issue_diagnosis.minimal_fix: Remove web UI from v1 scope entirely. Slack is the only interface. All output is Slack messages. Web UI ships when retrieval is proven valuable.

issue_by_issue_diagnosis.diagnosis: Vector embeddings require an embedding model call per incident, a vector store or pgvector setup, similarity threshold tuning, and evaluation. None of this is necessary to prove the retrieval behavior works.
issue_by_issue_diagnosis.minimal_fix: Deterministic scoring only: service_name exact match weighted highest, keyword overlap on symptom_text as secondary signal. Ship this. Embeddings are v2 after deterministic retrieval is validated.

conditions: LLM layer removed from v1 entirely.
Web UI removed from v1 entirely.
Pattern reinforcement implemented as simple increment/decrement with no decay algorithm.
All design decisions scoped to on-call engineer persona only.
Kill criteria defined and accepted before build begins.
final_classification.assessment: The core loop — intention capture, deterministic retrieval, action logging, artifact generation — is a Slack bot with five database tables and eight endpoints. That is a 48-hour build. The product document describes a correct and focused problem. The implementation risk is entirely in scope creep from adjacent features that are well-defined but not required for the first working loop.

paired_workflow.name: design_compiler
paired_workflow.action: Transform the raw idea into the leanest end-to-end system.
paired_workflow.output: Slack bot with five tables, eight endpoints, and a deterministic retrieval loop. No LLM. No web UI. No embeddings. Intention capture → retrieval → action log → artifact → pattern weight update.

paired_workflow.name: reality_filter
paired_workflow.action: Falsify the design against solo, free-tier, 48-hour constraints.
paired_workflow.output: Five issues identified. LLM layer, web UI, embeddings, six-persona scope, and premature reinforcement engine all cut or compressed. System reclassified as conditionally shippable.

paired_workflow.name: compression_loop
paired_workflow.action: If it fails, compress scope until it becomes shippable.
paired_workflow.output: Post-compression scope: /tc start, /tc action, /tc close. Deterministic retrieval. Artifact written to Postgres. Pattern weights as a simple counter. Slack only. One persona. This ships.

paired_workflow.name: build_order
paired_workflow.action: Produce the fastest implementation checklist for the first working loop.
output: Hour 0–2: Supabase project. Five tables. Schema locked.
Hour 2–6: FastAPI service. POST /incident, POST /intention, POST /action, POST /artifact, POST /reinforce.
Hour 6–10: GET /retrieve. Deterministic service + keyword scoring. Returns top 3 past incidents.
Hour 10–16: Slack bot. /tc start handler. Intention prompt. Retrieval injected into thread.
Hour 16–22: /tc action handler. /tc close handler. Artifact generation from template. Pattern weight update.
Hour 22–30: Deploy to Railway free tier. Internal smoke test with synthetic incidents.
Hour 30–48: First real incident logged. Artifact reviewed. Kill criteria evaluated.

shared_constraints.required_signal: An on-call engineer receives historically grounded first-check suggestions before exploring any diagnostic branch. That is the observable behavior change.

shared_failure_signals: LLM added to v1 retrieval path — scope leak, remove it
Web UI scoped into v1 — solo tax, defer it
Embeddings scoped into v1 — premature intelligence, defer it
More than one user persona driving v1 design decisions — false progress, cut to on-call engineer only
Decay algorithm built before 20+ real incidents exist — behavior vacuum, no data to decay
Cannot demonstrate retrieval output to a real engineer in 48 hours — ship criterion failed
